compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Validation note (decomposition review): the 'push.py already handles these conflicts today, so this only formalises it' framing (inherited from the epic) is slightly understated. push.py:148-175 handles .bridge_state/* only as a DIRTY-WORKING-TREE case (stash->merge->pop); a genuine COMMITTED-content conflict on bindings.json falls through to push.py:177-182 ('real content conflict -> merge --abort'). The tickets branch currently has NO .gitattributes. So WU-3 is net-new, non-redundant work that the WU-2 union merge genuinely needs to converge — not a pure formalisation.

compiled_state.description:
## What

Add a tickets-branch `.gitattributes` declaring `merge=ours` for the per-pass
mutable shared-root files so the union merge introduced in WU-2 never wedges on
them. uuid-named ticket dirs never collide; only `.bridge_state/bindings.json`
and the reconciler lock/gate files are rewritten every pass and would conflict.
The loser is a derived cache the reconciler rebuilds next pass — never ticket
events. `merge=union` is WRONG here (it line-unions JSON into invalid JSON).

This formalises behaviour `push.py` already handles ad hoc today, so it is a safe
standalone change and lands before WU-2 so the merge is conflict-free by construction.

## Scope (files)

- Tickets-branch `.gitattributes` (written/ensured at init): 
  `.bridge_state/* merge=ours` and `.reconciler-* merge=ours`.
- `src/rebar/_commands/init.py`: ensure the `.gitattributes` is created/maintained
  idempotently on the tickets branch.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tickets-branch `.gitattributes` ships `merge=ours` for `.bridge_state/*` and `.reconciler-*`
- [ ] `.gitattributes` is created idempotently at init (re-runnable)
- [ ] `merge=union` is NOT used (would corrupt JSON)
- [ ] Convergence test: two clones each rewriting `bindings.json` merge without a wedge
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green
compiled_state.title: WU-3: .gitattributes merge=ours for shared mutable root files
